metadata.title: Loshon HaTorah / Loshon Hakodesh Rule Candidates
metadata.source_policy: Supplemental enrichment only. Zekelman 2025 remains canonical.
metadata.source_warning: Raw PDFs are now present, but OCR, Hebrew, nikud, table layout, and exact source-page matching still require human review.
metadata.runtime_policy: These candidates are not runtime-ready and not question-ready.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 1 / DK-NOUN-001 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Recognize implied English indefinite article with simple Hebrew nouns
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: A simple Hebrew noun may translate naturally with English 'a/an' even when no separate Hebrew article is written.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Supports cautious noun translation and translation precision for taught noun vocabulary.
rule_candidates.notes: Useful for 3.01 and 3.04 only after source wording is verified.

raw_source_evidence.page_or_section: main extracted page 81 / printed p. 160 summary, citing pg. 2
raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Summary states that Hebrew has no word or letter for English 'a'.

rule_candidates.source_verification_note: Raw PDFs are now present, but Hebrew/OCR/table/layout review remains required before any diagnostic or question-generation planning use.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 1 / DK-ARTICLE-001 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Identify definite ×” as 'the'
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: A prefixed ×” before a noun usually adds the meaning 'the', while context and word identity still matter.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Clarifies basic definite article recognition for prefix/article review.
rule_candidates.notes: Does not cover all Zekelman 3.06 article subtypes or nikud shifts.

raw_source_evidence.page_or_section: main extracted page 81 / printed p. 160 summary, citing pg. 2
raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Summary states that ×” at the beginning of a noun means 'the'.

rule_candidates.source_verification_note: Raw PDFs are now present, but Hebrew/OCR/table/layout review remains required before any diagnostic or question-generation planning use.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 1 / DK-CONJ-001 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Identify prefixed ×• as 'and'
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: A prefixed ×• can function as a conjunction meaning 'and', but initial ×• should not be stripped blindly.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Supports visible prefix identification in 3.06 while leaving ×• ×”×”×™×¤×•×š unresolved.
rule_candidates.notes: The available rule does not provide direct support for ×• ×”×”×™×¤×•×š.

raw_source_evidence.page_or_section: main extracted page 81 / printed p. 160 summary, citing pg. 3
raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Summary states that ×• at the beginning of a noun means 'and'.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 1 / DK-GENDER-001, DK-PLURAL-001, DK-PLURAL-002, DK-PLURAL-003 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Recognize noun gender, number, and common plural endings
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: Hebrew nouns can be masculine or feminine, common plurals include ×™× and ×•×ª, and some feminine nouns ending ×” change form before plural ×•×ª.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Supports 3.04 noun-feature review and later source-checked form work.
rule_candidates.notes: Treat as pattern support only; exceptions and irregular forms still need teacher review.

raw_source_evidence.page_or_section: main extracted page 81 / printed p. 160 summary
raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Summary covers noun gender markers and common plural endings with exceptions.

raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Exercises and answers ask whether nouns are singular or plural and work through noun-ending forms.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 2 / DK-SMICHUS-001 and DK-SMICHUS-004 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Identify ×¡×ž×™×›×•×ª as a construct phrase
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: Two nouns can stand together as a construct unit where the first often means 'the ___ of', while some forms stay unchanged.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Directly enriches 3.04 smichut review and partially supports 3.08 phrase grouping.
rule_candidates.notes: This is strong enrichment for phrase-structure review but still lacks page verification.

raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Explains that two nouns side by side can convey 'the __ of __'.

raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Exercises and answers practice construct-form translation.

rule_candidates.source_verification_note: Raw PDFs are now present, but Hebrew/OCR/table/layout review remains required before any diagnostic or question-generation planning use.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 2 / DK-SMICHUS-002 and DK-SMICHUS-003 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Recognize common form changes in ×¡×ž×™×›×•×ª
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: Some construct forms change final ×” to ×ª, and final ×™× can change to ×™ in construct phrases.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Clarifies a form-sensitive part of 3.04 and supports cautious phrase grouping in 3.08.
rule_candidates.notes: Do not generalize beyond the modeled examples without raw-source and teacher review.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 2 / DK-SMICHUS-005 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Preserve ambiguity in possible construct phrases
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: Two-noun sequences may form an 'of' phrase or may require another contextual reading, so ambiguity should be preserved.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Supports caution around 3.04 smichut and 3.08 phrase-translation decisions.
rule_candidates.notes: Especially useful as a review warning, not as an active question pattern.

raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Missing-word discussion around ×‘×¨××©×™×ª illustrates why construct-like phrase translation can require context.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 1 / DK-WORDORDER-001 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Recognize variable verb and noun order
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: Hebrew can place the verb before or after the noun; students should identify the role of each word before translating.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Direct enrichment for 3.08 grouping and word-order review.
rule_candidates.notes: Good support for the review question about whether 3.08 can become a distinct diagnostic planning lane.

raw_source_evidence.page_or_section: main extracted page 4 / printed p. 6, Lesson 1
raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Explains that Torah wording can place the verb before or after the noun.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 1 / DK-WORDORDER-002 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Reorder Hebrew word order naturally in English translation
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: Students may need to translate Hebrew word order into natural English while preserving the Hebrew meaning.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Direct enrichment for 3.08 word-order and translation-construction review.
rule_candidates.notes: Supports review planning only; not a student-facing question template.

raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Explains translating literal Hebrew word order into natural English order.

rule_candidates.skill_label: Recognize full-word prepositions
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: The modeled lesson includes full-word prepositions such as ××œ, ×¢×œ, ×¢×, ×œ×¤× ×™, ××—×¨×™, and ×ž×Ÿ.

rule_candidates.skill_label: Recognize common prefix prepositions and ×©
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: The modeled lesson treats ×œ, ×‘, ×ž, ×›, and ×© as common prefixed function letters with distinct meanings.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Clarifies visible prefix/preposition work in 3.06.
rule_candidates.notes: Does not cover every nikud-dependent prefix shift in Zekelman 3.06.

rule_candidates.skill_label: Distinguish functions of ××ª-family forms
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: The modeled rules distinguish ××ª as a direct-object marker, ××ª meaning with, suffix forms attached to ××ª, and rare/context-sensitive readings.
example_words_if_safe: ××ª ×”××™×© -> the man (object marker not translated directly)

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 4 / DK-POSSESSIVE-001 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Identify possessive suffixes on singular nouns
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: Singular nouns can take suffixes that indicate ownership such as my, your, his, her, our, and their.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Directly enriches 3.05 pronominal suffix decoding as a separate lane from referent tracking.
rule_candidates.notes: The suffix table requires raw-source verification before use.

raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Exercises and answers work through possessive noun endings.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 4 / DK-POSSESSIVE-002 and DK-POSSESSIVE-003 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Parse plural possessive noun forms carefully
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: Plural nouns often add ×™ before possessive suffixes, and singular/plural possessive forms can look similar after suffixes are added.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Strengthens 3.05 suffix-decoding review and cautions against over-simple suffix lanes.
rule_candidates.notes: Useful as a boundary note for future diagnostic planning.

raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Summary states plural nouns with endings have a ×™ and distinguishes similar-looking singular/plural possessive forms.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 3 / DK-PARSE-001 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Use person, gender, and number labels in analysis
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: The modeled lesson uses person, gender, and number labels when analyzing Hebrew forms.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Supports 3.05 pronoun categories and 3.07 person/number/gender verb clues.
rule_candidates.notes: Does not resolve referent-tracking scope by itself.

raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Answer explanations label person, gender, and number in prepositions, verbs, and present-tense forms.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 1 / DK-VERB-001 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Match verb agreement to noun gender and number
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: The modeled rule says a verb should match the controlling noun in gender and number, and students must identify the noun controlling the verb.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Supports foundational 3.07 tense/person/form planning and 3.08 role-aware grouping.
rule_candidates.notes: Agreement examples are useful, but advanced binyan/passive areas remain outside this evidence.

raw_source_evidence.page_or_section: main extracted page 4 / printed p. 6 and page 81 summary
raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Explains that the verb agrees with the noun and warns against mismatched gender forms.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Lesson 5 / DK-PARSE-002 / raw PDF evidence linked separately; exact source wording still needs review
rule_candidates.skill_label: Use shoresh plus added letters to read verb features
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: To understand a verb, identify the shoresh and then read added letters for tense, person, number, and gender.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Supports 3.02 simple shoresh plus 3.07 foundational verb-feature planning.
rule_candidates.notes: The modeled rule itself warns against blind letter removal and irregular overgeneralization.

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Main book extracted page 81 / printed p. 160 summary; answer booklet extracted page 12 / Lesson 6
rule_candidates.skill_label: Treat weak-letter root behavior as a later reviewed boundary
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: The raw sources discuss weak letters that can drop out of or be replaced within verb roots, but this is advanced/root-sensitive evidence and should remain separate from simple shoresh identification.
rule_candidates.grade_or_level_hint: Main book summary cites pgs. 43, 82-83, 90, 138-139; answer booklet Lesson 6
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Clarifies the teacher-reviewed boundary that weak-letter and altered-root recognition should be deferred from foundational 3.02/3.07 planning.
rule_candidates.notes: Useful as a boundary marker only. Hebrew examples are OCR-sensitive and should not be reused without direct source review.

raw_source_evidence.page_or_section: main extracted page 81

rule_candidates.page_or_section: Main book extracted page 81 / printed pp. 160-161 summary; answer booklet extracted pages 25 and 31-32
rule_candidates.skill_label: Keep vav hahipuch as context-sensitive tense evidence
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: The raw sources discuss a ×•-form that can shift past/future tense readings and depends on sentence context, especially in narrative sequence.
rule_candidates.grade_or_level_hint: Main book summary cites pgs. 51-52 and 140-143; answer booklet Lessons 14-16
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Supports later review of 3.07 tense recognition and reinforces that vav hahipuch is not a simple visible-prefix diagnostic lane yet.
rule_candidates.notes: This supports a deferred/context-sensitive boundary, not a question-ready prefix or tense template.

raw_source_evidence.page_or_section: main extracted page 81
raw_source_evidence.evidence_summary: Summary says the form can change past-tense verbs to future and future-tense verbs to past, with context determining use.

rule_candidates.skill_label: Recognize present-tense form evidence as later-level verb enrichment
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: The answer booklet repeatedly identifies present-tense forms and sometimes explains missing letters or context-sensitive English rendering, but the main-book source pages need direct verification.
rule_candidates.grade_or_level_hint: Answer booklet Lessons 12-16; cited main-book pages include 101, 104-105, and 108
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Adds supplemental evidence for later 3.07 tense/form planning, while preserving the boundary that advanced verb lanes stay deferred.
rule_candidates.notes: Answer-booklet support is useful for reviewers, but this remains lower confidence until the cited main-book pages are checked directly.

rule_candidates.skill_label: Treat command forms as a separate later verb lane
rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: The answer booklet identifies command forms in translation exercises, but this evidence should remain a later reviewed lane and not be bundled into foundational verb-feature planning.
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Supports the existing teacher-review boundary that command forms belong in later 3.07 review, not foundational planning.
rule_candidates.notes: Do not use this as an active command-form template; source examples and level fit need teacher review.

rule_candidates.rule_or_activity_summary: The answer booklet notes forms whose vowel pattern changes at the end of a sentence or in pausal form, which may enrich later nikud review but does not resolve Zekelman 3.10 scope.
rule_candidates.grade_or_level_hint: Answer booklet cites main-book p. 96 in several notes
rule_candidates.relationship_to_chumash_skill: Provides limited support for keeping 3.10 nikud-sensitive work in a deferred review lane that needs direct source and Hebrew verification.
rule_candidates.notes: This strengthens the unresolved 3.10 review queue but does not make nikud reading question-ready.
